title: Treeline vertical family phase
summary: Fiftieth structured packet for Eco Explorer. This lane-3 wave strengthens the new treeline ascent into a fuller vertical loop and then gives tundra one compact terrain-relief proof so vertical identity extends beyond forest.

execution_order.why_now: Treeline now has the start of a vertical identity that should become more intentional.
A stronger treeline loop will keep lane 3 from over-centering forest.
The loop needs to stay readable and shelter-oriented.
A small tundra relief beat is the next best way to broaden vertical identity.
Even one compact tundra relief beat can make the inland half feel more varied.
The proof should stay calm and readable at the current scale.

guardrails: Keep the spaces cozy and recoverable.
Reuse the current tiny cue language.
Keep tundra's proof compact and terrain-first.
Avoid a harsher traversal shell or denser HUD.
acceptance_focus: Treeline gains a fuller vertical identity.
Tundra gets a small but real relief beat.
Lane 3 grows in biome variety without getting harsher.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-3.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-treeline-vertical-family-phase.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 3's next wave: strengthen the treeline ascent into a fuller vertical loop, then add one compact tundra relief proof while keeping the spaces cozy and recoverable.
